item.title: BlackRock Says Increased Downgrades Looming for State Borrowers
item.description: The market for state and local government debt is becoming “less forgiving,” according to BlackRock Inc.’s Pat Haskell, who leads the firm’s municipal bond group.

item.title: Blackstone Drops $4 Billion New World Deal Over Control Clash
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has walked away from a proposed $4 billion tie-up with New World Development Co. after the embattled property developer refused to hand over the reins, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Transnet to Allow 11 Firms to Operate Trains on Its Rail Lines
item.description: South Africa’s state transport firm Transnet SOC Ltd. granted 11 private companies concessions to operate trains on its freight-rail lines, part of a reform aimed at easing logistical bottlenecks that have constrained economic growth.

item.title: Mideast Markets Diverge as Investors Place Premium on Resilience
item.description: The Iran war has changed how global investors view the Middle East, with geopolitical resilience now outweighing economic growth as a draw for funds.

item.title: Tencent Revenue Miss Heightens Pressure for AI Payoff
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd.’s March-quarter revenue missed estimates, underscoring the urgency around its costly AI pivot while core businesses like games and advertising show signs of age.

item.title: Trump Repeats War Threats Against Iran Ahead of Meeting With Xi
item.description: US President Donald Trump repeated his military threats against Iran ahead of a visit to China, the Islamic Republic’s largest oil customer and a key diplomatic partner.

item.title: Forint Drops After Hungarian Central Bank Cuts Rate on FX Swaps
item.description: The forint weakened after the Hungarian central bank unexpectedly lowered the interest rate on its foreign-currency swaps following one of the biggest emerging-market currency rallies this year.

item.title: Japan Megabanks Set to Win Mythos Access After Bessent Visit
item.description: Japan’s three megabanks are set to secure access to Anthropic’s artificial intelligence model Mythos, according to a person familiar with the matter, after its limited release last month sparked fears of a new age of cybersecurity risks.

item.title: Turkey Depleted Reserves at Record Pace in March Over Iran War
item.description: Turkey’s foreign reserves had their biggest monthly decline on record in March, as the Iran war triggered global selloffs in emerging market assets and strained the lira.

item.title: Italy Says Suspected Hantavirus Case Is Negative, Easing Worries
item.description: A 25-year-old man placed in isolation in Italy tested negative for hantavirus, easing concerns about transmission on a flight that briefly hosted an infected Dutch woman.

item.title: BYD in Talks With Stellantis and Others About Taking Europe Plants
item.description: BYD Co. is negotiating with Stellantis NV and other European carmakers about taking over underutilized factories in the region, according to the Chinese company’s top international executive.

item.title: Samanth Subramanian on the Undersea Cables That Keep the Internet Alive
item.description: Listen to Odd Lots on Apple PodcastsListen to Odd Lots on SpotifyWatch Odd Lots on YouTubeSubscribe to the newsletterIn 2006, then-Senator Ted Stevens coined an infamous term for how to understand the internet: It's a "series of tubes." The funny thing is, that's a fairly accurate description. Underneath the world's oceans, miles and miles of fiber-optic cables send packets of information from one location to the next, serving as the backbone of the internet as know it. This infrastructure is de

item.title: Odd Lots: The Undersea Cables That Connect the World (Podcast)
item.description: In 2006, then-Senator Ted Stevens coined an infamous term for how to understand the internet: It’s a “series of tubes.” The funny thing is, that’s a fairly accurate description. Underneath the world’s oceans, miles and miles of fiber-optic cables send packets of information from one location to the next, serving as the backbone of the internet as know it. This infrastructure is delicate, too: Memorably, a 2022 volcanic eruption cut off the island of Tonga from web access for an extended period o

item.title: Nissan Forecasts Profit in Sign Restructuring is Taking Root
item.description: Nissan Motor Co. forecast an operating profit well above consensus estimates, an early indication that cost-cutting measures are helping to alleviate the carmaker’s cash-strapped financial position.

item.title: China AI Stocks Surge as Huang’s Visit Boosts Nvidia Supply Bets
item.description: Shares of Chinese AI model developers surged, reflecting bets that Jensen Huang joining President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing may help widen access to Nvidia Corp.’s advanced chips.

item.title: Anduril Valued at $61 Billion in Round Led by Thrive, Andreessen
item.description: Anduril Industries Inc. is being valued at $61 billion in a $5 billion funding round led by Thrive Capital and Andreessen Horowitz, the defense technology company announced on Wednesday.

item.title: Uzbek State Fund Rises in London Debut After $604 Million IPO
item.description: Uzbekistan’s national investment fund rose in its London trading debut, after the government sold a $604 million stake.

item.title: Greece Weighs Further Early Repayment of Bailout Era Loans
item.description: Greece is looking into repaying even more of its bailout loans ahead of schedule this year, a person familiar with the matter said.

item.title: ECB’s Rehn Says Data Show First Sign of Stagflationary Shock
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Olli Rehn warned that data are starting point to stagflation as a result of the Iran war and rising energy prices.

item.title: India FX Buffer Is Still Robust to Defend Rupee, Economists Say
item.description: India’s foreign-exchange reserves remain strong enough to defend its currency against the Iran-war driven oil shock, with buffers well above stress levels seen during the taper tantrum, according to economists.

item.title: Intertek Inclined to Recommend EQT’s Final £9.2 Billion Offer
item.description: Intertek Group Plc is leaning toward recommending a fourth and final takeover offer from private equity firm EQT AB.

item.title: Japanese Funds Dump Most US Debt Since 2022 as Fed Wagers Flip
item.description: Japanese investors sold the most US sovereign bonds in almost four years as a jump in oil prices led to an abrupt turnaround in Federal Reserve policy bets.

item.title: SoftBank Logs Surprise Profit Jump in Boon for Son’s AI Bets
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. reported a surprise rise in quarterly profit helped by valuation gains on its OpenAI investment.

item.title: Modi’s Belt-Tightening Push Raises Pessimism Around India Stocks
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s push to curb fuel and gold consumption has added to uncertainty in India’s already weak stock markets, raising concerns that moves meant to protect the economy may end up cooling overall spending.

item.title: Iran-Linked LPG Tanker Sails Past US Navy’s Blockade Line
item.description: A liquefied petroleum gas carrier, which has previously ferried Iranian cargoes, has sailed past the blockade boundary announced last month by the US Navy.

item.title: India’s RBI Governor Sees Fuel Price Hike If Oil Stays High
item.description: India may need to increase retail fuel prices if the conflict in the Middle East drags on, Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra said.

item.title: Hitachi Construction Shares Fall After $570 Million Block Trade
item.description: Hitachi Construction Machinery Co. shares extended their drop on Wednesday after major shareholder Hitachi Ltd. offloaded ¥90.1 billion ($571 million) of stock in a block trade.

item.title: FirstRand Picks BofA, RMB to Advise on Sale of UK Aldermore Unit
item.description: FirstRand Ltd. hired Bank of America Corp. and its own investment-banking arm, Rand Merchant Bank, as advisers to help it sell its UK-based Aldermore Group and exit the European market, a spokesperson for the South African lender said.

item.title: UK Bonds Recover Ground After Bruising Selloff
Korea President Clarifies Policy Chief’s ‘Citizen Dividend’ Post
item.description: Korean President Lee Jae Myung said his adviser’s viral post about redistributing profits from the AI boom was meant to start a broader public debate about how a potential tax windfall should be used.
